arts.ttl: Villa held by Juventus after last-minute Rogers winner ruled out
arts.sum: Watch highlights as Aston Villa are held at home by Juventus in the Champions League after a last-minute Morgan Rogers winner is overturned by VAR.

arts.ttl: Emery has tools to fix Aston Villa but overloaded Watkins needs support | Jonathan Liew
arts.sum: Hosts’ struggle to break down Juventus showed extent of the burden being shouldered by England forward So. A football match definitely happened. This much at least we can be sure of. I have a lanyard, a programme and a set of cryogenically frozen fingers to verify that fact. Other people were here too, I think. I can sort of remember noises. Vague rasping noises. Disappointed noises. The noise you make when you’ve paid £97 to watch Federico Gatti make back-passes. But already the actual memories of the event are beginning to evaporate, like a quick-drying paint, like the last thing you see before you go under general anaesthetic. Did Emiliano Martínez do something? Was Alessandro Del Piero on the pitch at some point? Hang on, it’s going blurry. Can no longer feel legs. Can no longer feel anything. Just floating. Orbs through space. Still floating. So nice here. So nice. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: MOTD pundits disagree over Aston Villa disallowed goal
arts.sum: Former Aston Villa midfielder Thomas Hitzlsperger, Julien Laurens and Danny Murphy disagree over whether Morgan Rogers' late goal against Juventus should have been overturned by VAR.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa’s disallowed goal would have counted in Premier League, claims Unai Emery
arts.sum: Villa were forced to settle for a 0-0 draw against Italian heavyweights Juventus

arts.ttl: Emery pleased with Villa's display against Juventus
arts.sum: Fixtures Aston Villa Vs. Juventus - Wed, Nov 27th 2024, 20:00 Unai Emery was pleased with Aston Villa’s performance after they played out a goalless draw with Juventus in the UEFA Champions League. Villa Park was bouncing once again as the hosts went head-to-head with the Italian giants, watching on as a tight encounter unfolded. Emi Martínez made a world-class save from Francisco Conceição at one end and Morgan Rogers saw a stoppage time winner ruled out for a foul on the visiting goalkeeper. Despite the late disappointment, Emery was proud of his team’s efforts. “It was fantastic,” he said. “We needed one match like this, playing seriously with a clean sheet, defending strong, being compact and then trying to impose and dominate some areas of the match. “We didn’t create a lot of chances but we didn’t concede a lot of chances. We were closer to scoring and in the second half we were progressively getting better. “We needed one save from Emiliano, a fantastic save, but even in the last action, in England it’s a goal, in Europe it’s not. That’s the decision of the referee. “The easier interpretation for the referee is a foul, but in England we know when the opponent is not using their hands to push the goalkeeper or to touch him clearly, usually it’s fair. We have to accept it. “We have ten points in the Champions League and we were enjoying it in Villa Park. We played a very serious match, a very competitive match against Juventus, respecting them. “I think we have to carry on enjoying this competition.” The draw moves Villa onto ten points in the league phase, placing them ninth in the table. The top eight qualify automatically for the next round, and Emery’s squad are chasing one of those spots as they approach their final three games. He said: “We are being very serious and competing very well. “We only conceded one goal in five matches. We have three wins and ten points. We have three matches to play and to enjoy. “We have possibilities and opportunities coming if we are winning to be a contender for the top eight positions. “The first objective is to get to the next round.”

arts.ttl: 'World number one' Martinez makes brilliant save against Juventus
arts.sum: Aston Villa goalkeeper Emiliano Martinez who was recently crowned best goalkeeper in the world at the Ballon d'Or awards makes a vital save for Aston Villa against Juventus in the Champions League.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa boss in 'very soft' claim after dramatic ending to Juventus stalemate
arts.sum: It was an even match and Emery believed a draw was a fair result after Emi Martinez made a stunning save to deny Francisco Conceicao just after the hour mark and John McGinn saw a goal-bound effort blocked by Manuel Locatelli. We were playing better and I think we more or less deserved to win the match than them.

arts.ttl: Fabrizio Biasin insists Motta is doing well and lists where Juventus’ problems lie
arts.sum: Juventus has experienced an inconsistent season under Thiago Motta, who is navigating challenging conditions in his debut campaign as… The post Fabrizio Biasin insists Motta is doing well and lists where Juventus’ problems lie appeared first on Juvefc.com.

arts.ttl: Juventus coach Thiago Motta: “We defended well but created little”
arts.sum: Following Juventus’ goalless draw against Aston Villa in the UEFA Champions League, head coach Thiago Motta shared his insights during a post-match interview. The match ended 0-0, reflecting a performance where defensive solidity was evident, but offensive creativity was lacking. Motta began by summarizing the match, stating, “We bring home a point. The team was compact; we left little space and created little. We played with great balance.” His remarks highlighted the team’s defensive efforts while acknowledging the need for improvement in their attacking play. arts.ttl: Pundits agree on Aston Villa VAR decision vs Juventus as Ollie Watkins reaction speaks volumes
arts.sum: Alan Hutton, Peter Crouch and Eniola Aluko all agreed that it was a harsh decision to disallow Aston Villa's goal against Juventus following the draw in the Champions League. Speaking on TNT Sports after the game, Aluko said of the decision to rule out the goal: "I think [Diogo] Carlos has absolutely every right to go for it, I actually think it's a goalkeeper error.
